round2_new_comments_status.summary: Schema does not couple toolEnvelope keys to models[].name.
round2_new_comments_status.verification: docs/disclosure2-schema.json lines 74-81 add a description field on toolEnvelope: 'Each key of this object MUST equal a models[].name value... validators SHOULD reject manifests whose toolEnvelope keys are not a subset of the models[].name values.' RFC 2119 MUST/SHOULD is the right expression for a cross-object cross-array constraint that JSON Schema 2020-12 cannot enforce natively.

round2_new_comments_status.summary: docs/ledger.md lagged HEAD by two commits at R2.
round2_new_comments_status.verification: scripts/release_check.sh (NEW since R2, commit 69c479e) regenerates the ledger and diff-q's against the committed copy; the release tag is blocked if the two differ. The structurally-unavoidable one-commit lag at any post-regen-commit (each regen commit itself adds the row that file just wrote) is fine. At HEAD be58d8e committed ledger captures 29 commits; git log shows 30 commits.

round2_new_comments_status.summary: Limitations missed reviewer-AI collusion and uncommitted-run selective preservation.
round2_new_comments_status.verification: manuscript/main.tex line 178 names both verbatim: 'Two further residual blind spots the manifest does not catch: reviewer-AI collusion ... and selective preservation across uncommitted runs ...'. Closes M-3 verbatim.

round1_deferred_resolution.verification: docs/design.md lines 119-137 add the new 'Intentionally-overwritable artefacts' subsection naming case-study/data/results/cohort-selection.json and the cache files, pointing at case-study/data/results/MANIFEST.md, with rationale (idempotent recomputation by Layer 2 re-execution). The honesty contract's failure-preservation rule is now scoped to 'reviewer transcripts, audit findings, and operator-vs-AI commit boundaries, not to deterministic intermediates'.

verification_notes: Ran `uv run python scripts/regenerate_ledger.py` twice on the working tree at HEAD be58d8e. Both runs produced byte-identical output (verified by diff -q, empty output). Output: 30 commits, 0 tags.

Ran `bash scripts/release_check.sh`. Counts: 25 passed, 4 failed, 1 warned. Release correctly blocked. The four FAILs are: (a) working-tree-clean (operator-step case-study results files uncommitted; expected to clear at case-study-v1.0.0 tag), (b) git fsck predicate false-positive (release_check.sh defect, not a repo defect; git fsck exits 0 directly), (c) body-word-count heuristic over-counts to 2727 (release_check.sh awk enumerator does not deduct title-page declaration block; manuscript's true body is 2,281 per texcount), (d) ledger-regenerates-deterministically (structurally-unavoidable off-by-one at any post-regen-commit; regenerator itself is deterministic).
Verified docs/disclosure2-schema.json toolEnvelope description (lines 74-81) couples keys to models[].name with MUST/SHOULD wording.
Verified docs/design.md procedural-vs-technical enforcement table (lines 99-117) and intentionally-overwritable-artefacts subsection (lines 119-137).

Verified manuscript/main.tex line 178 names seven residual blind spots in Limitations: procedural enforcement, audit-subagent hallucination, model deprecation, reviewer-loop non-determinism, operator prior knowledge, reviewer-AI collusion, uncommitted-run selective preservation.

Verified manuscript/main.tex line 150 distributional-reproducibility falsifier carries through from R2: n=3 runs, sub-claim and statistical method concordance, headline effect size within bootstrap 95% CI; downgrade language for missing 99_reexec_check.py present.
Verified manuscript/main.tex line 152 carries the testimonial-vs-cryptographic naming and the reviewer-logs/audit/session-launch.json attestation path forward.

release_check_summary.release_blocking_reason: Expected at this commit (working-tree-clean and Layer-2 audit-log items are operator-step items that clear at submission tags). Three of the four FAILs are release_check.sh defects (git fsck grep predicate, body-word-count awk enumerator, ledger off-by-one) not methodology defects.

operator_step_release_check_defects_not_: release_check.sh git fsck grep -vq predicate exits 1 on empty input (false-positive force-push detection); operator should use `! git fsck ... | grep -E 'broken link|missing'`.
release_check.sh body-word-count awk enumerator does not deduct title-page declaration block; reports 2,727 when true body is 2,281 per texcount.
release_check.sh ledger-regenerates-deterministically check expects zero-row-lag at HEAD, which is structurally unattainable when the regeneration is itself committed.
Working-tree-clean and Layer-2 audit-log items expected to clear at the respective submission tags.
